description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four rusted metal hoops. The wood displays a natural grain pattern with dark streaks, giving it an aged and weathered appearance. The metal bands are evenly spaced and show signs of oxidation, adding to the rustic character. The top is open, revealing the hollow interior, and there are no visible handles or lids. The surface of the wood appears rough and matte, while the metal hoops have a slightly corroded texture. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor or storage settings, such as alleys, warehouses, or gardens.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops and an open top, showing natural wood grain and a rustic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and all four metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with similar features, highlighting the curvature and texture of the wood.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, emphasizing the symmetry and even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Final view showing the opposite side, with consistent wood grain and metal banding.
